title: Short-TTL in-memory PSI result cache module (LRU, fail-soft, success-only)

files_forbidden: any component, route handler, or message catalog

acceptance_criteria: A self-contained in-memory LRU cache module (NO Redis — explicitly out of scope for this delivery) exposing a small API: get(key), set(key,value), and a wrapper helper the lighthouse call will use in T-06. No external/process dependency; safe to import in the scan-worker process.

Cache key is composed from normalized(targetUrl) + strategy ('mobile'|'desktop') ONLY. The category set is invariant today — do NOT key on it; add a code comment stating this and to revisit if onlyCategories is ever wired through.
Hard ENTRY-COUNT cap of 200 with LRU eviction (oldest/least-recently-used evicted first). A test inserts 10,000 distinct keys and asserts cache size never exceeds 200.
TTL is configurable via env PSI_CACHE_TTL_MS, default 300000 (5 minutes). An expired entry behaves as a miss (forces a fresh fetch). Test covers hit, miss, and expiry.

A force-refresh / bypass option is supported by the API (e.g. an option flag on the wrapper) so an explicit user-initiated re-scan can skip the cache entirely; test covers bypass returning a fresh value and not serving the cached one.

Fail-soft: any internal cache error in get/set must be swallowed and result in a normal miss (caller proceeds to live fetch); the cache NEVER throws to its caller. Test simulates an internal failure and asserts the wrapper still returns the live value.

Only successful results are cacheable: the module provides a way for the caller to NOT cache failure/UNAVAILABLE/empty-metrics responses (e.g. set is only called on success, or set rejects a sentinel). Test asserts a failure/UNAVAILABLE value is not served from cache on the next call.

Over-long normalized URL keys (beyond a sane cap) are not cached (skipped) rather than stored. Documented note that per-URL keying means an attacker can only affect their own URL's entry (no cross-URL poisoning).
npm run lint and npm run test pass for the new files.
output_format: code plus a plain-English explanation of the LRU cap, TTL choice (5 min vs fix-then-rescan), fail-soft, and the no-cache-on-failure rule
